steps.title: Build a two-row table
steps.content: The point $(0,5)$ is the $y$-intercept. A slope of $\frac{1}{4}$ means that when $x$ increases by $4$, $y$ increases by $1$, so another convenient point is $(4,6)$.

steps.title: Fit the line in Desmos
steps.content: Use $y_1 \sim mx_1+b$. Desmos gives $m=0.25$ and $b=5$, which is $f(x)=\frac{1}{4}x+5$.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content: The fitted line has slope $\frac{1}{4}$ and intercept $5$. Choice A matches both. Choice B has intercept $\frac{1}{5}$, choice C has intercept $-\frac{5}{4}$, and choice D has intercept $-5$. Choice A is correct.
